title: Hangover Be Gone: Top 10 Herbal Essential Oils for Relief

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for hangover because it helps reduce inflammation and nausea in the body, and also eases digestive issues caused by excessive drinking.
Shogaol is good for hangover because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nausea, vomiting, and headaches caused by excessive drinking.
Zingiberene is good for hangover because it helps reduce inflammation and soothe the stomach, which can alleviate symptoms such as nausea and headaches associated with a hangover.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oil, also known as ginger oil, is known to help with hangover symptoms. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help ease nausea and stomach discomfort often felt after a night of drinking too much. Ginger oil can also help calm the digestive system and reduce vomiting. This is because it has a compound called gingerol, which blocks the production of chemicals that trigger nausea and vomiting. Additionally, ginger oil has been shown to reduce oxidative stress, which can help alleviate headaches and fatigue associated with hangover. When inhaled, ginger oil can also help to relieve stress and anxiety, making it easier to recover from a hangover.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal essential oil for hangover relief, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried Avena sativa seeds or leaves, a carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil, and a clean glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid. Begin by infusing the carrier oil with the dried Avena sativa plant material in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring occasionally for about 2-3 hours, or until the oil has taken on the color and scent of the plant. Next, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the glass bottle, discarding the solids. Let the oil cool and mature for a few days before using it. For hangover relief, apply a few drops of the Avena sativa essential oil to the skin, or inhale it through steam inhalation, to help alleviate symptoms such as nausea, headaches, and fatigue associated with hangovers.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for hangover

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for hangover because it helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which are often caused by excessive drinking and can lead to hangover symptoms.
Isorhapontigenin is good for hangover because it helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which are common causes of hangover symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.

Bilobalide is good for hangover because it has antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which can alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.
Kaempferol is good for hangover because it helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can help to alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches and nausea.
Ginkgolide B is good for hangover because it helps to block the action of a chemical called bradykinin, which can make hangover symptoms worse and cause pain, inflammation, and swelling in the body.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oil is believed to help with hangover symptoms. The oil contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are thought to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. This can help alleviate headaches and fatigue, common issues experienced after a night of heavy drinking. Ginkgo biloba is also known to have antioxidant properties, which may help neutralize the damage caused by free radicals in the body after a night of drinking. Additionally, some people believe that the oil can help improve memory and cognitive function, which can be impaired after a hangover. The oil may also help reduce nausea and vomiting, making it a potential remedy for those suffering from hangover symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal essential oil, also known as oat kernel oil, which helps with hangovers, you'll need a few basic supplies. First, you'll need to source high-quality Avena sativa seeds, which are rich in saponins and other compounds that help soothe the stomach and calm the body. Next, dry and grind the seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder. Then, transfer the powder to a clean glass jar and cover it with a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or jojoba oil, making sure that the seeds are completely submerged. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the oil penetrate the seeds. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your Avena sativa herbal essential oil is now ready to use - simply apply a few drops to your skin or inhale the oil to help alleviate hangover symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oils for hangover

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Valerenic acid is good for hangover because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce headaches and nausea caused by excessive drinking.

Valeranone is good for hangover because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce headaches and nausea, and also helps to calm the nervous system, which can ease the overall symptoms of a hangover.
Valerenal is good for hangover because it helps to reduce hangover symptoms like nausea and headaches by acting as a natural sedative and anti-inflammatory.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oil, also known as valerian oil, is a natural remedy that may help alleviate hangover symptoms. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to calm the mind and body. The oil contains a compound called valerenic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These properties can help ease headaches, muscle cramps, and nausea associated with hangovers. Valerian oil may also help regulate sleep patterns, which can be disrupted due to excessive drinking. When inhaled, the oil's sedative effects can calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress caused by hangovers. Additionally, valerian oil has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal essential oils for hangover relief, start by sourcing high-quality Avena sativa seeds or leaves. Harvest the seeds when they are mature and dry, or use the leaves from a mature plant. Dry the material thoroughly to prevent any moisture from causing spoilage. Next, use a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil to extract the beneficial compounds from the Avena sativa. Fill a glass jar with the dried seeds or leaves, and then pour the carrier oil over them. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally. This process is called maceration and allows the beneficial compounds to infuse into the carrier oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids. Finally, filter the oil again to remove any remaining impurities, and your Avena sativa herbal essential oil is ready to use. You can apply a few drops to your skin, inhale it, or add it to a bath for relief from hangover symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal essential oils for hangover

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for hangover because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the pain, swelling, and discomfort caused by a hangover.
Menthol is good for hangover because it helps to ease nausea and headaches by reducing inflammation and improving blood circulation, making it easier to feel better after a night of heavy drinking.
Menthone is good for hangover because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce the pain and discomfort caused by hangovers.

Luteolin is good for hangover because it helps reduce inflammation and block the action of a chemical called prostaglandin, which is released when we drink too much and can make hangover symptoms worse.
Linalool is good for hangover because it helps reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive system, which can alleviate symptoms of nausea and headaches that often occur after excessive drinking.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint essential oil, is a popular natural remedy for hangover symptoms. It works by targeting the stomach and digestive system. Peppermint oil can help ease nausea and stomach cramps, making it easier to keep food down. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties may also reduce headache pain and body aches associated with hangovers. Additionally, peppermint oil can help increase blood flow and reduce stress, which can contribute to feelings of fatigue and grogginess.  To use peppermint essential oil for hangover relief, it's best to inhale the scent directly from a cloth or inhale it through steam inhalation. Some people also add peppermint oil to a warm bath or use it in aromatherapy diffusers. When using peppermint oil, always dilute it with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, as it can be too strong on its own.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal essential oils for hangover relief, you will need some basic equipment and ingredients. First, you need to dry and prepare Avena sativa flowers, which can be bought from a herbal store or grown in your garden. Dry the flowers thoroughly and then crush them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder. Next, fill a clean glass jar with 2-3 cups of dried flower powder and pour 1 liter of high-quality carrier oil, such as jojoba or sweet almond oil, over it. Allow the mixture to steep in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar gently every day, until the oil has taken on the color and aroma of the flowers. After the steeping period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discard the solids, and your Avena sativa herbal essential oil is ready to use. You can apply it topically to the skin, inhale it through steam inhalation or add it to your bath water to help alleviate hangover symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for hangover

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for hangover because it helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can alleviate symptoms such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue caused by excessive alcohol consumption.

Quinic acid is good for hangover because it helps to reduce inflammation, calm the nervous system, and replenish the body's stores of antioxidants, which can help to alleviate symptoms such as headaches and fatigue associated with hangovers.
Quinine is good for hangover because it helps reduce inflammation and pain in the body, making it easier to recover from a night of drinking.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis essential oil is a natural remedy that may help alleviate hangover symptoms. This oil is derived from the Cinchona tree, a plant native to South America, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The main compound in Cinchona officinalis essential oil is quinine, a natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant that can help reduce hangover symptoms such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue. Quinine works by blocking the pain pathways in the brain, which can help ease headache pain and other discomforts associated with hangovers. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis essential oil may also help to reduce inflammation and promote digestion, which can help alleviate nausea and stomach discomfort often experienced after a night of heavy drinking. When inhaled, the oil's vapors can also help to calm the mind and reduce stress, which can contribute to the relief of hangover symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal essential oil that helps with hangover, start by gathering dried oat straw, also known as Avena sativa. This herb is known for its calming and soothing properties, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms. Begin by infusing the dried oat straw in a carrier oil, such as jojoba or sweet almond oil, in a clean glass jar. Fill the jar about 1/4 full with the dried oat straw, then pour the carrier oil over it, making sure that the oil completely covers the herb. Next, seal the jar and place it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the infusion process. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. The resulting oil can be used topically, added to a bath, or inhaled to help alleviate hangover symptoms, such as headaches and nausea.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for hangover because it has antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and protect the body from damage caused by excessive drinking, making it a potential remedy to alleviate hangover symptoms.
Quercetin is good for hangovers because it helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, making it easier to recover from the damage caused by excessive drinking.

Apigenin is good for hangover because it helps to reduce inflammation, soothe the stomach, and calm the nervous system, making it easier for the body to recover from the effects of excessive alcohol consumption.
Luteolin is good for hangover because it helps reduce inflammation and pain caused by excessive drinking, and also protects the brain from damage by preventing the release of toxins.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oil, also known as lemon balm oil, is a natural remedy that can help alleviate hangover symptoms. It contains compounds like rosmarinic acid and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties can help reduce inflammation in the body and neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to cells. As a result, lemon balm oil may help ease headache and nausea associated with hangovers. Additionally, the oil has a calming effect on the mind and body, which can help alleviate anxiety and stress that often accompany hangovers. It may also help regulate the digestive system and reduce vomiting. People who have used lemon balm oil for hangover relief report that it helps them feel better and recover faster.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal essential oils for hangover relief, start by harvesting or purchasing dried oat straw or oat tops, which are the parts of the Avena sativa plant used for medicinal purposes. Clean and dry the oat material thoroughly to prevent contamination. Next, place the oat material in a clean glass jar, covering it with a carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil. The general ratio is to use 1 part oat material to 3-4 parts carrier oil. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally to allow the oil to infuse into the carrier oil. This process is called maceration. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the oat material. You can then use the Avena sativa essential oil in a diffuser or blend it with other essential oils for a hangover-relief remedy. However, please note that essential oils are highly concentrated and should be used with caution and in small amounts.

Luteolin is good for hangover because it helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which can alleviate symptoms such as headaches and nausea associated with a hangover.

Α-Bisabolol is good for hangover because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce headache and nausea caused by excessive drinking, also it has antioxidant properties that help protect the liver from damage.
Quercetin is good for hangover because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress and soothe the stomach, making it easier to recover from a night of heavy drinking.

remedies.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla essential oil, also known as German chamomile oil, is a natural remedy that can help with hangover symptoms. It contains compounds like bisabolol and chamazulene, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. When taken orally or applied topically, it can help to calm the stomach, reduce nausea and vomiting, and ease headaches. This is because the oil's active ingredients can help to relax the digestive system and ease muscle spasms, which can be beneficial for people experiencing stomach discomfort after a night of heavy drinking. Additionally, the oil's calming effects can also help to reduce anxiety and stress, which can be a major contributor to hangover symptoms. By using Matricaria chamomilla essential oil, individuals may be able to alleviate some of the unpleasant symptoms associated with hangovers and feel more comfortable after a night of excessive drinking.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal essential oil for hangover relief, start by harvesting or purchasing dried oat straw, which is the herb's common name. Next, dry the oat straw further to reduce its moisture content, and then chop it into smaller pieces to release its active compounds. Combine the dried oat straw with a carrier oil, such as jojoba or sweet almond oil, in a ratio of about 5-10% essential oil concentration. This means if you use 100ml of carrier oil, you'll add about 5-10ml of the dried oat straw. Allow the mixture to steep for 2-4 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the container occasionally, to allow the active compounds from the oat straw to infuse into the carrier oil. After the infusion period, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the oil from the solids, and then discard the solids. Finally, store the Avena sativa essential oil in a dark glass bottle, away from direct sunlight, and use it topically or add it to a bath for relief from hangover symptoms, such as nausea and headaches.
remedies.image_alt: Matricaria chamomilla herbal essential oils for hangover

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Curcumin is good for hangover because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce headaches, nausea, and fatigue caused by excessive drinking.
Polyphenols are good for hangover because they have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation, relax muscles, and replenish vitamins and minerals in the body.

Demethoxycurcumin is good for hangover because it helps reduce inflammation, blocks the action of a compound that causes hangover symptoms, and has antioxidant properties that repair damage to the body caused by excessive drinking.
Tannins are good for hangover because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the swelling in the brain and body caused by excessive drinking.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oil, also known as turmeric oil, has been used for centuries to help alleviate hangover symptoms. It contains a powerful compound called curcumin, which is a natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant. When you drink too much alcohol, it can cause inflammation in your body, leading to headaches, fatigue, and nausea. Curcumin in turmeric oil helps to reduce this inflammation, which in turn can ease hangover symptoms. Additionally, turmeric oil has been found to have a protective effect on the liver, which can become damaged when you drink too much alcohol. This means that using turmeric oil after a night out can help to minimize the negative effects of hangovers and support your body's natural recovery process.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal essential oils for hangover relief, you'll need Avena sativa seeds and a few basic equipment. First, dry the Avena sativa seeds in a warm and dry place or by using a food dehydrator, until they become brittle and crumbly. Next, grind the dried seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Then, mix the Avena sativa powder with a carrier oil, such as coconut or jojoba oil, in a 1:10 ratio. For example, mix 1 gram of Avena sativa powder with 10 grams of coconut oil. Add a few drops of vitamin E oil to prevent oxidation and spoilage. Now, place the mixture in a dark glass bottle and let it steep in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the bottle occasionally. After the steeping period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another dark glass bottle, and discard the solids. Your Avena sativa herbal essential oil is now ready to use. To use it for hangover relief, simply massage a few drops of the oil onto your temples, wrists, or behind your ears before going to bed, or apply it topically to the affected areas in the morning.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal essential oils for hangover

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for hangover because they have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and protect the brain cells from damage caused by excessive drinking.
Polyphenols are good for hangover because they help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which can alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.
Tannins are good for hangover because they help reduce inflammation and soothe the stomach, which can be irritated by the toxins that build up in the body after excessive drinking.

Kaempferol is good for hangover because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the damage to the body caused by excessive drinking, and also help alleviate symptoms such as headaches and nausea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata essential oil, also known as passionflower oil, is a natural remedy for hangovers. This is because it has properties that help to calm the mind and body, reducing the symptoms of a hangover. It contains a compound called flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to ease the pain and discomfort caused by a hangover. Additionally, passionflower oil has a sedative effect, which can help to reduce anxiety, stress, and irritability that often come with a hangover. It can also help to regulate the digestive system, which can become upset after consuming too much alcohol. Moreover, passionflower oil has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce the oxidative stress caused by the toxins in the body after a night of drinking. By using passionflower essential oil, you can help to alleviate the symptoms of a hangover, such as nausea, headaches, and fatigue.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal essential oils that help with hangover, start by gathering a few stalks of Avena sativa, also known as oat straw. Dry the stalks in a warm, dark place for 1-2 weeks to remove excess moisture. Once dry, chop the stalks into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar. Add a carrier oil, such as jojoba or sweet almond oil, to cover the chopped oat straw completely. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally to help the oil extract the beneficial compounds from the oat straw. After the infusion period, strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. The resulting oil can be stored in a cool, dark place and used as needed to help alleviate hangover symptoms, such as nausea and headaches, due to its calming and soothing properties.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Avenanthramides are good for hangover because they have antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and damage to cells in the brain and body caused by excessive drinking.
Saponins are good for hangover because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the swelling and pain in the head and body caused by alcohol consumption.
Flavonoids are good for hangovers because they help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the brain, which can help to alleviate headaches and other hangover symptoms.

Ferulic acid is good for hangover because it has antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and damage caused by excessive drinking, and also has anti-nausea effects that can help alleviate hangover symptoms such as stomach discomfort and dizziness.

Tannins help alleviate hangover symptoms because they have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce blood vessel swelling, improve liver function, and reduce oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa essential oil is believed to help with hangover symptoms. This oil comes from the oat plant and has been used for centuries in herbal medicine. When you drink too much alcohol, it can disrupt your body's balance of fluids and electrolytes. Avena sativa oil may help to calm your stomach and reduce nausea, which is a common hangover symptom. It's thought to have anti-inflammatory properties, which could help to reduce the severity of a hangover headache. Additionally, Avena sativa oil may help to stabilize your mood and reduce anxiety, which can be helpful when you're feeling queasy and irritable after a night of drinking. Some people also find that it helps to replenish their body's stores of vitamin B, which can become depleted when you drink too much alcohol. By applying Avena sativa oil topically or inhaling it through steam inhalation, you may be able to alleviate some of the unpleasant symptoms of a hangover.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal essential oils that help with hangover, start by harvesting or purchasing dried Avena sativa flowers and leaves. Next, dry and clean the plant material thoroughly to prevent any contamination. Then, fill a clean glass jar with the dried plant material and cover it with a carrier oil, such as jojoba or sweet almond oil, leaving about an inch at the top. Seal the jar and place it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the plant material infuse into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the plant material. Finally, add a few drops of the Avena sativa essential oil to a diffuser or mix it with a carrier oil for topical use, as it is believed to help alleviate hangover symptoms by soothing the digestive system and reducing inflammation.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal essential oils for hangover

intro_desc: When you have a hangover, your body is feeling the effects of too much alcohol. Herbal essential oils can help you feel better. There are two herbs that are good for hangovers: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, and Ginkgo biloba. Ginger is good because it helps to calm your stomach and reduce nausea. When you drink too much, your stomach can get upset and you might feel queasy. Ginger oil can help to soothe your stomach and make you feel more comfortable. Ginkgo biloba is good because it helps to improve blood flow to your brain. When you're hungover, your brain can feel foggy and it's hard to think clearly. Ginkgo biloba oil can help to increase blood flow and make you feel more alert.  Fixing the issue of hangovers is important because it can affect your daily life. If you have a hangover, you might not be able to work or take care of your responsibilities. You might also feel tired and irritable, which can affect your relationships with your family and friends. Using herbal essential oils can help you to recover from a hangover more quickly and feel better sooner. To use ginger oil, you can add a few drops to a bath or inhale it from a diffuser. To use Ginkgo biloba oil, you can add a few drops to a bath or massage it into your skin. You can also mix a few drops of both oils together and inhale them from a diffuser. This can help you to feel more relaxed and focused, and recover from your hangover more quickly.
supplementary_best: The best combination of herbal essential oils to remedy hangover is a blend of Zingiber officinale (Ginger), Avena sativa (Oat), and Passiflora incarnata (Passionflower) essential oils. Zingiber officinale helps to alleviate nausea and stomach discomfort due to its anti-inflammatory properties. Avena sativa soothes digestive issues and calms the nervous system to reduce hangover symptoms. Passiflora incarnata, rich in antioxidants, reduces inflammation and anxiety, which is often associated with hangovers. This combination is effective because it targets multiple aspects of hangover symptoms, including nausea, digestive issues, and anxiety. The anti-inflammatory properties of Zingiber officinale and Passiflora incarnata help to reduce inflammation in the body, while Avena sativa's calming effect on the nervous system reduces stress and anxiety.
supplementary_avoid: If you have a hangover, it's best to avoid essential oils from certain herbs that can make symptoms worse. For example, you should avoid oils from 'Juniperus communis' (Juniper) as they can increase urination, which may lead to dehydration and make your hangover worse. 'Sassafras albidum' (Sassafras) oil is also not recommended because it can cause stomach problems, which are already a concern with a hangover. 'Cassia auriculata' (Cassia) oil may cause allergic reactions or interact with other medications, so it's best to avoid it when you're not feeling well.
supplementary_other_remedies: When it comes to hangovers, some people turn to natural remedies that don't involve herbal essential oils. One option is coconut water, which helps replenish fluids and electrolytes lost during drinking. It's rich in potassium, an essential mineral that can become depleted after a night of partying. Drinking coconut water the next morning can help alleviate hangover symptoms like headaches and fatigue.  Another remedy is ginger, but not in the form of essential oil. Instead, people make ginger tea by steeping fresh ginger in hot water. The active compound in ginger, called gingerol, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the stomach and reduce nausea. Some people also drink ginger ale or ginger beer, but be careful not to confuse these with actual ginger tea. The carbonated drinks can make hangover symptoms worse.  Some people also rely on electrolyte-rich beverages like sports drinks, like Gatorade or Pedialyte. These drinks contain a mix of essential minerals like sodium, potassium, and calcium that can become imbalanced after heavy drinking. Drinking sports drinks can help rebalance these minerals and alleviate hangover symptoms like headaches and muscle cramps.  Other people swear by drinking apple cider vinegar the morning after a night out. Apple cider vinegar contains acetic acid, which can help balance the body's pH levels and alleviate hangover symptoms like nausea and stomach cramps. Some people also drink it mixed with water or juice to make it more palatable.  Lastly, some people rely on rehydrating with plain water, but adding a slice of lemon or lime can make it more flavorful. The acidity in citrus fruits can help stimulate digestion and alleviate hangover symptoms like nausea and stomach cramps.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have a hangover often experience headaches, nausea, and fatigue. Medicinal herbs like feverfew and willow bark can help alleviate headaches. Feverfew contains a compound called parthenolide, which reduces inflammation and stops pain signals to the brain. Willow bark contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin that helps to ease pain and reduce fever.  Ginger and peppermint can help with nausea. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe the stomach and reduce vomiting. Peppermint has a calming effect on the stomach and can help to ease nausea and bloating.  For fatigue, herbs like ginseng and ashwagandha can be helpful. Ginseng is a natural energy booster that helps to reduce fatigue and improve mental clarity. Ashwagandha is an adaptogen that helps the body to adapt to stress and can reduce fatigue and anxiety.  Licorice root can help with dehydration, a common symptom of hangover. It has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation in the body.
